edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese crime family ... is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the "Five Families" that dominate organized crime activities in New York City and New Jersey as part of the American Mafia.
edges.context: Genovese is one of the Five Families.

edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese crime family ... is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the "Five Families" that dominate organized crime activities in New York City and New Jersey as part of the American Mafia.
edges.context: Genovese is part of the American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the Philadelphia, Cleveland, Patriarca, and Buffalo crime families.
edges.context: Genovese has ties with Philadelphia crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the Philadelphia, Cleveland, Patriarca, and Buffalo crime families.
edges.context: Genovese has ties with Cleveland crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the Philadelphia, Cleveland, Patriarca, and Buffalo crime families.
edges.context: Genovese has ties with Patriarca crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the Philadelphia, Cleveland, Patriarca, and Buffalo crime families.
edges.context: Genovese has ties with Buffalo crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese crime family originated from the Morello gang of East Harlem, the first Mafia family in New York City.
edges.context: Genovese family originated from the Morello gang.

edges.evidence_quote: One of Giuseppe Morello's strongest allies was Ignazio "the Wolf" Lupo, a mobster who controlled Manhattan's Little Italy. In 1903, Lupo married Morello's half-sister, uniting both organizations.
edges.context: Morello and Lupo were allies and united through marriage.

edges.evidence_quote: The Morellos had an alliance with Giosue Gallucci, a prominent East Harlem businessman and Camorrista ... The fight over Gallucci's rackets became known as the Mafia-Camorra War.
edges.context: Morello initially allied with Gallucci (Camorra) but later fought the Camorra in the Mafia-Camorra War.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1920, both Morello and Lupo were released from prison and Brooklyn Mafia boss Salvatore D'Aquila ordered their murders.
edges.context: D'Aquila ordered the murders of Morello and Lupo.

edges.evidence_quote: This is when Giuseppe "Joe" Masseria and Umberto Valenti, an enforcer working for Salvatore D'Aquila, began to fight for control of the Morello family.
edges.context: Masseria fought for control of the Morello family.

edges.evidence_quote: By the late 1920s, Masseria's main rival was boss Salvatore Maranzano, who had come from Sicily to run the Castellammarese clan. Their rivalry eventually escalated into the bloody Castellammarese War.
edges.context: Masseria and Maranzano's Castellammarese clan fought the Castellammarese War.

edges.evidence_quote: In a secret deal with Maranzano, Luciano agreed to engineer Masseria's death in return for taking over his rackets and becoming Maranzano's second-in-command.
edges.context: Luciano initially allied with Maranzano to betray Masseria.

edges.evidence_quote: By September 1931, Maranzano realized Luciano was a threat, and hired Vincent "Mad Dog" Coll, an Irish gangster, to kill him.
edges.context: Maranzano plotted to kill Luciano.

edges.evidence_quote: Wary of Genovese gaining more power in the Commission, Gambino used the Apalachin meeting as an excuse to move against his former ally.
edges.context: Gambino moved against Genovese, leading to Genovese's conviction.

edges.evidence_quote: In the late 1960s, Gambino lent $4 million to Eboli for a drug scheme in an attempt to gain control of the Genovese family.
edges.context: Gambino lent money to Genovese front boss Eboli to gain control.

edges.evidence_quote: When Eboli failed to pay back his debt, Gambino, with Commission approval, had him murdered in 1972.
edges.context: Gambino had Genovese front boss Eboli murdered over debt.

edges.evidence_quote: After the 1980 murder of Philadelphia boss Angelo "Gentle Don" Bruno, Gigante and Lombardo began manipulating the rival factions in the war-torn Philadelphia family. They finally gave their support to Philadelphia mobster Nicodemo "Little Nicky" Scarfo, who in return gave the Genovese mobsters permission to operate in Atlantic City in 1982.
edges.context: Genovese supported Scarfo in Philadelphia war, gaining Atlantic City access.

edges.evidence_quote: Gambino, Luciano, Costello, and Lucchese allegedly lured Genovese into a drug-dealing scheme that ultimately resulted in his conspiracy indictment and conviction.
edges.context: Lucchese conspired with Gambino to set up Genovese.

edges.evidence_quote: On August 4, 2016, the United States Attorney of New York charged 46 Mafia leaders with racketeering conspiracy... The 46 defendants were leaders, members, and associates of the Genovese, Gambino, Luchese, Bonanno, and Philadelphia crime families.
edges.context: Genovese and Bonanno members were charged together in a conspiracy.

edges.evidence_quote: In October 2017, thirteen Genovese and Gambino associates and soldiers were sentenced after being indicted following an NYPD operation in December 2016.
edges.context: Genovese and Gambino members were jointly indicted and sentenced in the same operation.

edges.evidence_quote: On August 13, 2020, an indictment charged soldier Christopher Chierchio along with Colombo family associate Frangesco "Frankie" Russo ... with conspiracy, wire fraud and money laundering.
edges.context: Genovese soldier and Colombo associate were indicted together for a fraud scheme.

edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family and the Bonanno family jointly operated several illegal gambling operations while using front businesses in Queens and Long Island to launder illegal profits.
edges.context: Genovese and Bonanno families jointly operated illegal gambling operations.

edges.evidence_quote: On June 6, 2023, five men with connections to the Genovese and Lucchese crime families were indicted for carrying out two brazen midday Manhattan jewelry heists.
edges.context: Men connected to Genovese and Lucchese families were indicted together for jewelry heists.

edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the Philadelphia ... crime families.
edges.context: Genovese family has ties with Philadelphia crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the ... Cleveland ... crime families.
edges.context: Genovese family has ties with Cleveland crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the ... Buffalo crime families.
edges.context: Genovese family has ties with Buffalo crime family.

edges.context: Genovese family has ties with Patriarca family.

edges.context: Genovese family has ties with Buffalo family.

edges.evidence_quote: In July 1992, Nichilo was indicted along with DeCavalcante crime family soldier Virgil Alessi, boss John Riggi and underboss John D'Amato for plotting to murder the DeCavalcante family's acting boss Gaetano "Corky" Vastola.
edges.context: Genovese capo Nichilo indicted with DeCavalcante members for plotting murder of DeCavalcante acting boss.

edges.evidence_quote: Tenaglia was initially affiliated with the Brooklyn faction of the Bonanno family, the Motion Lounge crew, which was formerly headed by Dominick "Sonny Black" Napolitano, and was transferred to the Genovese family.
edges.context: Genovese soldier Tenaglia was previously affiliated with Bonanno family before transferring.

edges.evidence_quote: In December 1993, Gargiulo was indicted for operating a sports betting ring reputedly worth $86 million annually in cooperation with the Gambino, Colombo and Lucchese families.
edges.context: Genovese soldier Andrew Gargiulo cooperated with Gambino family in a sports betting ring.

edges.evidence_quote: In December 1993, Gargiulo was indicted for operating a sports betting ring reputedly worth $86 million annually in cooperation with the Gambino, Colombo and Lucchese families.
edges.context: Genovese soldier Andrew Gargiulo cooperated with Colombo family in a sports betting ring.

edges.evidence_quote: In December 1993, Gargiulo was indicted for operating a sports betting ring reputedly worth $86 million annually in cooperation with the Gambino, Colombo and Lucchese families.
edges.context: Genovese soldier Andrew Gargiulo cooperated with Lucchese family in a sports betting ring.

edges.evidence_quote: In April 2005, Colletti was indicted for illegal gambling, along with 16 other defendants associated with the Genovese and Bonanno families.
edges.context: Genovese soldier Victor Colletti and Bonanno soldier Anthony Frascone led an illegal gambling operation together.

edges.evidence_quote: In August 2016, Conigliaro was one of 46 defendants linked to the Genovese, Gambino, Lucchese, Bonanno and Philadelphia families indicted for multiple racketeering offenses.
edges.context: Genovese soldier Alex Conigliaro was indicted alongside members of the Philadelphia family in a multi-family racketeering case.

edges.evidence_quote: In May 2011, Palumbo was sentenced ... in a plot of conspiracy to murder an individual associated with the Russian Mafia.
edges.context: Genovese soldier Anthony Palumbo conspired to murder a person associated with the Russian Mafia, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: On August 16, 2022, Polito was indicted along with Genovese family soldier Joseph Macario, Genovese family associates Salvatore Rubino and Joseph Rutigliano, Bonnano family capo Anthony "Little Anthony" Pipitone, Bonanno family soldier Vito Pipitone, Bonanno family associate Agostino Gabriele and Nassau County Police Detective Hector Rosario.
edges.context: Members of both families were indicted together for racketeering and other charges, indicating cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: In June 2023, DiPietro and associates of the Lucchese and Gambino families, Michael Sellick, Vincent Spagnuolo, Vincent Cerchio, and Samuel Sore, were arrested for stealing $2 million worth of jewellery
edges.context: Genovese soldier Frank DiPietro and associates of the Lucchese family were arrested together for a jewelry theft.

edges.evidence_quote: In June 2023, DiPietro and associates of the Lucchese and Gambino families, Michael Sellick, Vincent Spagnuolo, Vincent Cerchio, and Samuel Sore, were arrested for stealing $2 million worth of jewellery
edges.context: Genovese soldier Frank DiPietro and associates of the Gambino family were arrested together for a jewelry theft.

edges.evidence_quote: since at least May 2012, he was the head of a lucrative illegal gambling operation in Lynbrook, New York in association with the Bonanno family, with capo Anthony "Little Anthony" Pipitone on behalf of the Bonanno family.
edges.context: Genovese soldier Joseph Macario ran an illegal gambling operation in association with the Bonanno family.

edges.evidence_quote: On September 7, 1916, Ubriaco along with Nicholas Morello meet with the Navy Street gang in Brooklyn and they both were shot to death on Johnson Street in Brooklyn.
edges.context: Morello family member Eugene Ubriaco and Nicholas Morello met with the Navy Street gang and were killed.

edges.evidence_quote: Valachi was active since the Castellammarese War during the early 1930s, as an associate of the Lucchese crime family; however; after the murder of Salvatore Maranzano in 1931, he joined the Genovese family.
edges.context: Valachi moved from Lucchese to Genovese, indicating a transfer of a member between families.

edges.evidence_quote: Along with Genovese and Gambino members, he received kickback payments.
edges.context: Genovese and Gambino members jointly received kickback payments, indicating cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: At a meeting with the Gambino crime family in the late 1960s, it was agreed upon that the Gambino family would own the Brooklyn and Staten Island waterfronts, and the Genovese family would control the Manhattan and New Jersey waterfronts.
edges.context: Genovese and Gambino families agreed to divide waterfront territories, a cooperative arrangement.

edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the Philadelphia, Cleveland, Patriarca, and Buffalo crime families.
edges.context: Genovese family has ties with Philadelphia crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the Philadelphia, Cleveland, Patriarca, and Buffalo crime families.
edges.context: Genovese family has ties with Cleveland crime family.

edges.context: Genovese family has ties with Patriarca crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Genovese family has also generally maintained a varying degree of influence over many of the smaller Mafia families outside New York, including ties with the Philadelphia, Cleveland, Patriarca, and Buffalo crime families.
edges.context: Genovese family has ties with Buffalo crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: In Boston, Massachusetts, the New England or Patriarca crime family from Providence, Rhode Island, has long dominated the North End of Boston, but has been aligned with the Genovese family since the Prohibition era.
edges.context: Patriarca crime family has been aligned with Genovese family since Prohibition.

edges.evidence_quote: Anthony Zoccolillo — former associate of the Genovese and Bonanno families
edges.context: Zoccolillo was an associate of both Genovese and Bonanno families, indicating a link between the families.
